sourceMovie.title: Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit for the same reason it broke through in 2014: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Kenneth Branagh keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Ultimatum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit clicked for you, The Bourne Ultimatum (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit?
faq.answer: Try The Bourne Ultimatum, The Bourne Supremacy, The Sum of All Fears—each shares DNA with Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit?
faq.answer: Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit (2014) is best described as Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
